questions.q: In the Puranas, Balarama is described as the elder brother of which deity?
questions.explanation: Balarama is celebrated in the Bhagavata Purana as the elder brother of Krishna.

questions.q: Which farming implement is the signature weapon and emblem of Balarama?
questions.explanation: Balarama is famed for bearing the plough (hala), which gives him the epithet Halayudha.

questions.q: In addition to the plough, which weapon does Balarama traditionally wield?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana describes Balarama bearing both the plough (hala) and the mace (gada).

questions.q: In many Puranic lists, Balarama is regarded as an incarnation of which cosmic serpent?
questions.explanation: Balarama is widely regarded as an incarnation of Shesha, the great serpent on whom Vishnu reclines.

questions.q: The Puranas describe Balarama as having which distinctive complexion?
questions.explanation: Balarama is described as fair in complexion, contrasting with his dark-hued brother Krishna.

questions.q: Balarama is portrayed as a devoted protector of which community in Vrindavan?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana presents Balarama as a strong protector of the cowherd (Gopa) community of Vrindavan.

questions.q: Which epithet, meaning 'one whose weapon is the plough', is given to Balarama?
questions.explanation: Balarama is called Halayudha, literally 'one whose weapon is the plough'.

questions.q: Balarama and Krishna are the sons of which father?
questions.explanation: Both Balarama and Krishna were born as sons of Vasudeva of the Yadava clan.

questions.q: In Vaishnava lists of the Dashavatara that include Balarama, whom does he most commonly replace?
questions.explanation: In many Vaishnava reckonings of the ten avatars, Balarama is listed in place of Buddha, while Krishna is counted as the eighth.

questions.q: Which Purana is a principal source for the life and deeds of Balarama?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana is a principal text describing the life, complexion, and weapons of Balarama.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, Balarama's embryo was transferred from Devaki to the womb of which woman?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana relates that the embryo was transferred to Rohini, making Balarama her son.

questions.q: Because of the miraculous transfer of his embryo, Balarama is sometimes given which name?
questions.explanation: From the 'drawing across' of his embryo, Balarama is also known as Sankarshana.

questions.q: In the Puranas, who is named as the wife of Balarama?
questions.explanation: Balarama's consort is Revati, the daughter of King Kakudmi (Raivata).

questions.q: Revati, the wife of Balarama, was the daughter of which king?
questions.explanation: Revati was the daughter of King Kakudmi, also called Raivata, of the Anarta region.

questions.q: Which demon, who had assumed the form of an ass, did Balarama slay in the Talavana grove?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana describes Balarama killing the ass-demon Dhenuka, who guarded the Tala forest.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, Balarama dragged which river closer to Vrindavan with his plough?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana relates that Balarama compelled the river Yamuna to come near by force of his plough.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, Balarama served as the teacher of mace combat to which two warriors?
questions.explanation: Balarama, a master of the mace, trained both Bhima and Duryodhana in the art of gada warfare.

questions.q: What stance did Balarama take during the great Kurukshetra war of the Mahabharata?
options: He remained neutral and went on pilgrimage
questions.explanation: Balarama declined to fight for either side and instead undertook a pilgrimage along the Saraswati during the war.

questions.q: Which beverage is Balarama particularly associated with in the Puranas?
questions.explanation: Balarama is associated with Varuni, a sweet liquor, which appears in several of his Puranic episodes.

questions.q: Balarama belonged to which clan or dynasty, the same as Krishna?
questions.explanation: Balarama was born into the Yadava clan, specifically the Vrishni line of Vasudeva.

questions.q: Whom did Balarama drag by his plough to widen the city of Hastinapura when his nephew was held captive?
questions.explanation: The Puranas relate that an enraged Balarama set his plough to the ramparts of Hastinapura to free his nephew Samba, the son of Krishna.

questions.q: Balarama is frequently depicted holding which drinking implement along with his weapons?
questions.explanation: In iconography Balarama is often shown holding a wine cup, reflecting his association with Varuni.

questions.q: In the Jain tradition, Balarama is classed among which category of heroic figures?
questions.explanation: Jain texts count Balarama (Baladeva) among the nine Baladevas, gentle heroes paired with the Vasudevas.

questions.q: Which tyrant king of Mathura did Balarama help overthrow alongside Krishna?
questions.explanation: Balarama accompanied Krishna to Mathura and assisted in the overthrow of the tyrant king Kamsa.

questions.q: During a wrestling match before Kamsa, which royal wrestler did Balarama defeat?
questions.explanation: At Kamsa's arena Krishna slew Chanura while Balarama overcame the wrestler Mushtika.

questions.q: According to the Bhagavata Purana, how did Balarama depart from the world at the end of his earthly life?
options: A great white serpent issued from his mouth and he merged with the sea
He ascended on a chariot of fire
questions.explanation: The Purana describes a white serpent, his true form as Shesha, emerging from his mouth as he left the world by the sea.

questions.q: In the Krishna lore, which monkey-king did Balarama once fight when fruit was withheld from him?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana relates that Balarama slew the wicked ape Dwivida, who had ravaged the countryside.

questions.q: Within the Pancharatra doctrine of the four vyuhas, which emanation of Vishnu is identified with Balarama as Sankarshana?
questions.explanation: In the four-vyuha system, Balarama is identified with the emanation Sankarshana, who is associated with the divine qualities of knowledge (jnana) and strength (bala).

questions.q: Balarama is recorded in the Mahabharata as having gone on pilgrimage along which sacred river during the war?
questions.explanation: The Mahabharata narrates Balarama's tirtha-yatra along the banks of the Saraswati while the Kurukshetra war raged.

questions.q: When Revati was found to be of a former age, how did Balarama adjust her stature to wed her, according to the Puranas?
options: He prayed to Brahma to restore her youth
He pressed her down with the tip of his plough
He gave her a boon of immortality
He carried her across the ocean
questions.explanation: The Puranas relate that Balarama touched Revati with his plough to bring her tall stature down to a suitable height.
